invent_allowed: Bar Cordano service details and Lima street color
which clerk or student points toward Trinidad
epilogue beats tying survivors toward Jackson Elias's later Carlyle trail
never_invent: that Larkin openly confesses serving Nyarlathotep at the opening dinner
a complete pyramid map that skips all Puno/Nayra routes without earned clues
that Nayra cheerfully joins the final ruins assault as a combatant
that holy water or ordinary prayer alone permanently destroys kharisiri while the ward remains broken
that de Mendoza is merely a mortal mercenary with no Mythos nature
releasing the Father of Maggots as a harmless scenic vision with no campaign cost

keeper_secrets.prose: M'Weru tattooed the Bloody Tongue on Larkin; Nyarlathotep uses him to break the Andean ward.
Luis de Mendoza is an intelligent kharisiri conquistador acting as Larkin's bodyguard and cult enforcer.
The Father of Maggots (El Padre de los Gusanos) is imprisoned under the pyramid; kharisiri regurgitate offerings into the cracked ward.
Repairing the golden ward severs the kharisiri from their god; the cult withers though myths linger.
Peru is a prologue: Elias's Larkin/kharisiri trail feeds into the Carlyle Expedition investigation that opens the main campaign.
